diff:
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index 25b45b9..319cbdb 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -1,5 +1,12 @@
 # Changelog
 
+## Unreleased
+
+- Fixed `Compress` middleware ignoring `q=0` quality values in the `Accept-Encoding` header.
+  `matchAcceptEncoding` now parses the `q` parameter and treats any encoding with `q=0` as
+  not acceptable (e.g., `Accept-Encoding: gzip;q=0, identity` no longer triggers gzip
+  compression).
+
 ## v5.0.12 (2024-02-16)
 
 - History of changes: see https://github.com/go-chi/chi/compare/v5.0.11...v5.0.12
diff --git a/middleware/compress.go b/middleware/compress.go
index 4e46f70..251820b 100644
--- a/middleware/compress.go
+++ b/middleware/compress.go
@@ -239,9 +239,23 @@ func (c *Compressor) selectEncoder(h http.Header, w io.Writer) (io.Writer, strin
 
 func matchAcceptEncoding(accepted []string, encoding string) bool {
 	for _, v := range accepted {
-		if strings.Contains(v, encoding) {
-			return true
+		v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
+		// Split on ';' to separate the encoding name from parameters (e.g., q=0).
+		parts := strings.SplitN(v, ";", 2)
+		name := strings.TrimSpace(parts[0])
+		if name != encoding {
+			continue
 		}
+		// Encoding name matched. Check for q=0 which means "not acceptable".
+		if len(parts) > 1 {
+			param := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
+			if strings.HasPrefix(param, "q=") {
+				if strings.TrimPrefix(param, "q=") == "0" {
+					return false
+				}
+			}
+		}
+		return true
 	}
 	return false
 }

exploration_log.aha_moment: The bug is in a single line: `strings.Contains(v, encoding)` doesn't handle `;q=0` because `strings.Contains("gzip;q=0", "gzip")` is true. The fix is to split on `;` first.
exploration_log.hindsight: No dead ends; the task description was precise about both the problem and the fix.

alternatives: Parse full RFC 7231 quality values including float q values
Only handle the q=0 special case
key_decisions.reasoning: The task description specifically asks to treat q=0 as not acceptable. Full quality-value sorting (q=0.5 vs q=0.9) is out of scope for this narrow fix.

solve_notes: Replaced the `strings.Contains(v, encoding)` check in `matchAcceptEncoding` with proper token parsing: split each Accept-Encoding token on `;`, compare the encoding name exactly, then check if a `q=0` parameter is present and return false if so. Also added a CHANGELOG.md entry in an 'Unreleased' section documenting the behavioral fix.
task_text:
Fix Compress middleware ignoring Accept-Encoding quality values

The `matchAcceptEncoding` function in `middleware/compress.go` uses
`strings.Contains` to check whether a requested encoding name appears
in the comma-separated `Accept-Encoding` header value. This does not
respect the `q` quality parameter: a client sending
`Accept-Encoding: gzip;q=0, identity` explicitly opts out of gzip, but
`strings.Contains("gzip;q=0", "gzip")` returns true and the response is
compressed anyway. Fix `matchAcceptEncoding` to split each token on `;`,
parse the `q=` parameter if present, and treat any encoding with `q=0`
as not acceptable. Add a `CHANGELOG.md` entry documenting this
behavioral fix.

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows how the accepted slice is built (strings.Split of Accept-Encoding header) and how matchAcceptEncoding is invoked, which is necessary to understand the token format

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains only the edited function. An AI agent would also read `selectEncoder` to understand how the `accepted []string` slice is constructed (each element is a raw token from the comma-split header), which confirms the right parsing approach. Without reading `selectEncoder`, the agent might guess at the input format and implement incorrect tokenization.
